blocks.text: To: A Plus Home Health Systems, LLC
The information contained in this fax transmission is intended only for the individual(s) named above.
Such information is confidential and may be legally privileged. If you have received this fax transmission
in error, please notify me immediately by using the telephone number provided above so that / may
arrange for this fax transmission to be returned to me or destroyed. If the recipient of this fax
transmission is not the individual(s) named above, such recipient is hereby notified that this fax
transmission may not be copied, disseminated, distributed or otherwise disclosed to others.
If unable to contact the sender of this fax, please contact the Privacy Officer, Bob Breighner, at 888-735-

Downing, Margaret A (MR # 7570 / ACCT # 10941) Printed at 3/30/2026 12:30 PM EDT
Reason for Admission: Functional deficits after
Primary Care Physician: PCP Not on Staff
Chief Complaint: Fall with a left pelvic and hip fracture
This is a 88-year-old female who states to me she lives with 3 adult man at home, 1 is her husband and 2 adult
She use walker for ambulation outside of the house inside the house she does not use anything. Patient had a
mechanical fall she was brought over to Saint rose Hospital found to have left pelvic ring fracture as well as left
acetabular fracture for which she follow up with Dr. Howard orthopedic and no surgical intervention needed.
She was given IV Dilaudid which she become delirious that was stopped.
At this time she is admitted in the rehab her mentation has improved.
Patient continues to require close physician management of patient's ongoing medical issues. Preceding
events led to patient's decline in function. Acute physical therapy and occupational therapy failed to return
patient back to prior level of function. Patient continues to require 24 hour medical supervision. Patient was
transferred to Dignity Health Rehabilitation Hospital on 03/16/2026.
History reviewed. No pertinent surgical history.
History reviewed. No pertinent family history.
Support System and Family Circumstances (i.e., potential caregivers): spouse / significant other and children /

Constitutional: No fevers, chills, sweats. Pleasant obese female sitting up in chair. States family is at home to
help and she would rather like to go home today.

Endocrine: Negative for excessive thirst, excessive hunger

Patient requires 15 hours of therapy weekly for gait, balance, and ADL deficits that cannot be adequately
treated at a lesser level of care. Patient requires 15 hours of therapy weekly for mobility, transfers, strength,
and ADL deficits that cannot be adequately treated at a lesser level of care. Patient requires 3 hours of therapy
daily for gait, balance, ADL deficits that cannot be adequately treated at a lesser level of care. Patient requires
3 hours of therapy daily for mobility, transfers, strength, and ADL deficits that cannot be adequately treated at a
lesser level of care. Patient requires 24 hour rehab nursing care due to bowel and/or bladder management, and
to maintain/improve skin integrity. Patient requires 24 hour rehab nursing care due to decreased personal care
skills, medication issues, and to reinforce team goals outside of therapy sessions.
Downing, Margaret A (MR # 7570 / ACCT # 10941) Printed at 3/30/2026 12:30 PM EDT
Code status has been reviewed with patient Patient is a not specified.
Modified Independent mobility, transfers, gait and ADLs.
Complete individualized plan of care without exception.
Return to PLOF with least restrictive device.
At the current time, this inpatient hospital rehabilitation stay is medically necessary to achieve important health
and functional goals. The patient requires frequent physician visits, 24-hour rehabilitation nursing, and a
coordinated intensive rehabilitation program as described above to address complex medical, nursing, and
rehabilitation needs. The patient has a good prognosis for benefiting from this program and returning home
Goals and plans discussed with patient and is in agreement.
Functional target: Modified Independent/ supervision level

ADL Training Narrative: Pt was seated in WC upon arrival and agreeable to tx. Therapist facilitated self
care activities to increase safety and independence with ADLs and functional transfers. Pt declined
shower but agreeable to hygiene and grooming and dressing. Pt also completed toileting with min a for
clothing management and LE dressing while seated on toilet with min a for threading B LE into pants
and use of reacher. Pt required extra time for LE dressing due to B LE edema. Pt also completed UE
dressing while seated on toilet with set up assist. Pt completed self care with hygiene and grooming
and self feeding independently while seated.
Patient Education: Pt reported that she had developed B LE blisters due to increased edema, pt also
reports that she does not sleep in bed at remained in WC all day and night. Therapist provided
education for edema reduction, elevation and postioning to decrease edema in B LE. Pt was provided
printout and all questions and concerns were addressed about postioning in bed and importance of
OT Narrative:: Pt was instructed in self care and edema education to increase safety and
independence with ADLs and functional transfers. Pt completed all toileting, dressing and hygiene and
grooming with good safety but extra time due B LE edema and fatigue. Pt also required min verbal
cues to maintain WB precautions. Pt remained in WC at completion of session with belt alarm on, call
light in reach and all needs met.
oT Treatment Outcomes:: Safety device reapplied, Patient tolerated treatment well and Medical issues
OT Summary Plan of Care: Continue with current plan of care
Response to Therapy Interventions: Improved positioning (03/29/26 0821)
Nursing notified of patient's pain assessment: Not indicated - pain score 2 or less (03/29/26 0821)

Transfer Level of Assistance 1: Moderate Assistance
Trials/Comments1: Mod A to initiate and complete pivot, increased cues for NWB maintenance; VC
for hand placement to increase IND and safety
Transfer Level of Assistance 2: Moderate Assistance
Trials/Comments 2: VC for NWB to LLE; pt highly dependent on grab bar; educated on other hand
placements d/t not having vertical grab bar in the home; VC for sequencing and safety; mod A to
perform transfer and steady during clothing management
Wheelchair Propulsion Level of Assistance: Close Supervision
Wheelchair Analysis: 80' + 100' with VC for proper propulsion and steering, increased time to
complete distance, increased time to complete turns
Mat Position: Supine SLR, heel slides, hip abduc, glute sets, SAQs; with AA as needed on LLE; VC
for proper form and control; performed for improving BLE strength as well as to improve active
muscel pump to decrease edema in BLE
Therapeutic Activities and Neuromuscular Re-education: Sit to stand in //bars: 5x holding as long as
tolerated ~15s at a time, VC for upright posture VC for NWB/assist for NWB compliance; performed
for improving IND of transfers, increasing standing tolerance, and increasing NWB compliance in
PT Treatment Outcomes:: Improved transfer ability noted, Improving overall functional endurance
noted, Increasing strength, Patient tolerated treatment well, Patient demonstrating carry-over of
learned techniques from prior sessions and Patient requires cueing to maintain precautions
PT Summary:: Pt continues to have fluid filled blisters in B feet and ankles as well as increased
edema. Pt reporting she has not been supine in days d/t discomfort and anxiety related to being in
bed. Pt however agreeable to bed level exercises with HOB elevated to improve tolerance.
Performed supine exercises with legs elevated to decrease edema through active muscle pump and
positioning; tolerates up to 30 minutes in supine before requiring positional change to bed.
Educated on attempting to get into bed more frequently throughout day to assist with swelling
reduction; switched w/c leg rests to elevating. RN providing pain medication at end of session per pt
request during therapeutic rest break. Pt left in w/c with legs elevated with alarm intact and all needs
met, call light and bedside table in reach. Pt would benefit from continued skilled intensive PT

I have reviewed all Imaging and testing from the last 24 hrs
Case of a 88 year female with past medical history significant for diabetes, hypothyroidism and CHF who
presented to the hospital for evaluation after a ground level fall. Patient was evaluated in the ER she was
found with left acetabular fracture/left pelvic ridge fracture. Patient was admitted to the hospital, Orthopedic
surgery was consulted Dr. Howard and recommended against any surgical intervention. Patient was stabilized
and now transferred to dignity rehab for continuation of care.
- Status post traumatic fall with associated left acetabular fracture/left pelvic ring fracture
Downing, Margaret A (MR # 7570 / ACCT # 10941) Printed at 3/30/2026 12:30 PM EDT

- Cont Wound care eval for LLE skin tear
- Currently on Lantus 40 units (decreased from 60 units) after episode of hypoglycemia
- Continue metoprolol /Entresto for BP control for BP control
-close BP monitoring and adjust therapy p.r.n.
- Recommend outpatient follow-up with orthopedic surgery
